description_long: This hanger consists of two sturdy vertical wooden posts connected at the top by a horizontal wooden beam, forming a rectangular frame. Four parallel metal rods or wires stretch between the two posts, providing ample space for hanging items such as clothes or tools. The posts are reinforced with diagonal wooden braces for added stability, and the overall construction appears robust and functional. The wood has a natural brown finish, while the rods or wires are metallic and likely made of steel or aluminum. The design is open and minimal, with no additional decorative elements or coverings. This type of hanger is commonly found in outdoor or semi-outdoor urban environments, such as backyards, parks, or communal laundry areas.
description: A robust wooden frame hanger with four parallel metal rods for hanging items, supported by diagonal braces. The design is open, minimal, and functional, suitable for outdoor urban use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the hanger, with both vertical posts and all four parallel rods clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the structure, the thickness of the wooden posts, and the diagonal braces supporting the frame.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the parallel rods and the wooden frame from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the side profile and structural braces.
